premise: After my husband died, I sold our $340,000 home and moved in with my daughter, using the sale money to pay off her debts on her promise of lifetime care, but six months later she forged a lease and locked me out, claiming I was a tenant. I sued for elder exploitation, proved the oral contract through text messages, and when she couldn’t pay the $410,000 judgment, I took ownership of her six‑bedroom house at auction; now I run it as a shared‑housing community for displaced older women, charging rent and sending her monthly invoices for the property tax she still owes.

thumb.label: Thumbnail + metadata
thumb.detail: My Daughter Forged Lease, Locked Me Out, And Stole My $340,000. So I Took Her Ho
